title: 60-Second Lead Response for Law Firms
metaDescription: Done-for-you 60-second lead response for law firms. Every inquiry answered in under a minute, in your voice, with your intake or booking link.
excerpt: Every new inquiry to your law firm gets a personal reply in under 60 seconds, in your voice, with your intake link. Installed and run for you.

sections.intro:
If you run a law firm, the first lawyer to reply usually wins the matter. Studies on inbound legal leads keep showing the same thing: response time beats brand, beats price, and often beats reviews. Yet most firms still take hours, sometimes a full day, to answer a contact form, a Google Local Services lead, or a referral email. By then the prospective client has called two other attorneys and signed with whoever picked up.

The AIOS 60-Second Lead Response is the done-for-you Capability that closes that gap. Every new lead, from any source you use, gets a personal reply in under 60 seconds. The reply sounds like you, references what they asked about, screens for conflicts and case type, and offers a clear next step (consult booking link, intake form, or a direct call). You stay in control. The system just makes sure nobody waits.
sections.fourCFit:
This page is a Capability inside the four-layer AIOS. It only works because the other three layers are already in place:

  • Context: your practice areas, jurisdictions, fee posture, intake questions, and the matters you will not take.
  • Connections: your inbox, web forms, lead sources, calendar, and case or CRM system, read live, not copied.
  • Capabilities: the 60-Second Lead Response itself, plus downstream drafts like the follow-up sequence and the intake summary for the attorney.
  • Cadence: the system runs 24/7, including evenings and weekends when most personal legal questions actually get asked.

The wedge starts here. Once the lead response is reliable, the same plumbing powers consult prep, conflict checks, no-show recovery, and weekly pipeline reporting.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When the install is finished, here is the artifact you own:

  • A reply sent in under 60 seconds, every time, on every channel you accept leads from.
  • Copy that sounds like the responsible attorney, not a chatbot, with your firm name, signature, and disclaimers.
  • A clear next step in every message: a booking link to a consult slot, an intake link, or a phone option for urgent matters.
  • Basic triage: practice area tag, urgency flag, obvious conflicts surfaced, and out-of-jurisdiction leads handled with a polite decline or referral note.
  • A short internal summary delivered to the attorney or intake coordinator so the human follow-up is informed, not cold.
  • SLA: 60 seconds or less from lead arrival to first reply, measured and reported weekly.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install on a fixed three-phase path. No long discovery, no surprise scope.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we map your current lead sources, response times, and intake steps, and confirm the firm is a fit. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we capture your voice, write the reply templates with you, connect the lead sources and calendar, set the triage rules, and test against real and sample leads until the SLA holds.
  3. Operate (ongoing): we run the system for you and add one new automation per week, such as consult reminders, no-show recovery, or matter intake summaries. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
Based on what we typically see at firms with 20 to 200 inbound leads per month:

  • Median first-reply time drops from 2 to 12 hours down to under 60 seconds.
  • Consult booking rate from web and directory leads commonly improves by 20 to 40 percent.
  • Intake staff stop living in the inbox after hours and on weekends.
  • Attorneys walk into consults with a clean summary instead of opening the email for the first time.
  • Standing guarantee: across the full AIOS, we recover 40 or more hours a month for the firm, or we keep working free until we do.
sections.faq:
Is this a chatbot that talks to clients on our behalf?

No. It is a reply system, not a negotiator. It sends the first message in your voice, screens basic information, and routes the lead to the right human. It does not give legal advice and does not pretend to be the attorney.

How do you keep it ethics-compliant and conflict-aware?

We bake your jurisdiction rules, disclaimers, and required language into every template, and we surface potential conflicts to a human before any commitment is made. You approve the copy before it ever goes live, and you can change it at any time.

What if a lead has a true emergency, like an arrest or a deadline today?

Urgent matters are flagged in the triage step and routed to a phone or pager path you define, not just an email. The 60-second reply still goes out, and a human is alerted immediately on the channel you choose.
